summary: Punchy China>Rider and rare Saint of Circumstance shine
blurb: The second set delivers a scorching China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, the seldom‑played 1988 Saint Of Circumstance, and an inspired Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale. Jerry’s vocals stay high‑energy while Brent’s Hammond work drives the jams.
review: October 14, 1988 at Miami Arena captures a late‑‘80s Dead show that swings hard in the second set. The first set is solid, highlighted by “Brown Eyed Women” and a bright “Bird Song,” but the real fireworks begin with the punchy China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue—bass bombs from Phil give it extra thrust while Brent’s organ swirls, even if he gets a bit busy at times. The rare 1988 rendition of Saint Of Circumstance feels fresh and adds historical weight to the set. From there the band rolls into an extended “He’s Gone” that feeds into a crisp Drums/Space segment before launching into the high‑energy “Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad” → “I Need A Miracle” → “Dear Mr. Fantasy.” The show closes on a sing‑along “Hey Jude Finale,” leaving the audience buzzing. Jerry’s guitar work is uneven at moments, but his vocal fire and tight harmonies keep everything cohesive; Bob’s lead vocals on “When I Paint My Masterpiece” are enthusiastic, and Mickey and Bill lay down solid, no‑frills drumming throughout. Overall, it’s a show with standout peaks that make it worth a listen despite occasional rough edges.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, two are mixed (neutral). The consistent praise for the China>Rider transition, Saint Of Circumstance, and Dear Mr Fantasy > Hey Jude across multiple sources yields a moderate positive sentiment adjustment of +1.0.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard with punchy bass, captures the standout China>Rider transition and rare Saint Of Circumstance while maintaining overall balance.

key_highlights: Punchy China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition with big bass bombs.
Rare 1988 performance of Saint Of Circumstance adds historical significance.
"Dear Mr. Fantasy" → "Hey Jude Finale" segue stands out as a memorable closing sequence.
Drums/Space segment is solid and flows smoothly into the second‑set jam.

band_performance.Jerry: Playing was uneven with some off‑kilter guitar moments, but his high‑energy vocals and tight harmonizing kept the show moving forward.
band_performance.Phil: Delivered abundant bass "bombs" that drove the first set, though a few listeners felt they were a bit excessive early on.
band_performance.Bob: Provided enthusiastic lead vocals on “When I Paint My Masterpiece” and maintained strong vocal presence throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Hammond work was generally strong and full‑bodied; occasionally got busy during the China>Rider transition but added excitement to the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivered solid, no‑frills drumming; the Drum/Space segment was highlighted as a reliable groove.
band_performance.Billy: Provided steady, well‑balanced drum work that complemented Mickey and kept the rhythmic foundation tight.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
